title: Neooreophilus werneri
intro:
I couldn't find information about a plant called "Neooreophilus werneri." However, I found a plant called "Notothylas werneri", which is a species of liverwort, a type of non-vascular plant. 

Here's a paragraph about Notothylas werneri:

Notothylas werneri is a species of liverwort, a small, non-vascular plant that belongs to the family Notothyladaceae. The health benefits of Notothylas werneri are not well-documented, but it is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Notothylas werneri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Notothylas werneri are not well-established, but it may be used in traditional medicine to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
